_doc: Doubles teambuilding archetype model (T-102). Same shape as singles.json: ONE base archetype (balanced|offensive|defensive) + a weighted, STACKABLE gimmick layer (Batch-3 owner decision — multi-gimmick teams are normal, e.g. weather+trapping or Tailwind+Trick Room). Tuned for our 6v6 doubles (Smogon DOU), NOT VGC 4v4 — see docs/research/6v6-vs-4v4-doubles.md + rating-decisions.md. The 2C engine reads `entry` for emergent crystallization and `structure` as soft preferences. PROVISIONAL per the meta-analysis validation clause.

featureDefinitions.intimidateUser: Intimidate (or Snarl/Fake Tears offensive stat-drop) support that blunts the foes
featureDefinitions.fakeOutUser: Fake Out user (turn-1 flinch, buys a setup/positioning turn)
featureDefinitions.spreadAttacker: carries spread damage (Rock Slide / Heat Wave / Blizzard / Muddy Water / Earthquake)
featureDefinitions.redirector: Follow Me / Rage Powder, or a redirection ability (Lightning Rod / Storm Drain)
featureDefinitions.wideGuardUser: Wide Guard / Quick Guard support vs spread / priority
featureDefinitions.trickRoomAbuser: slow, high-offense mon that benefits from Trick Room
featureDefinitions.choiceScarfRevengeKiller: Choice Scarf revenge-killer / speed control (a DOU staple)
featureDefinitions.regeneratorPivot: bulky Regenerator pivot (premium in 6v6 — heal-on-switch scales with game length)
featureDefinitions.pivotUser: U-turn / Volt Switch / Flip Turn pivot (re-applies Intimidate, builds momentum)
featureDefinitions.weatherSetter: sets weather via ability (Drizzle/Drought/Sand Stream/Snow Warning) or move
featureDefinitions.weatherAbuser: Swift Swim / Chlorophyll / Sand Rush / Slush Rush, or a weather-boosted spread nuke
featureDefinitions.trapper: Shadow Tag / Arena Trap / Magnet Pull (remove-the-counter — allowed gimmick, Batch-3)
featureDefinitions.perishSongUser: Perish Song (pairs with a trapper for Perish Trap)
featureDefinitions.setupSweeper: boosts then sweeps (Belly Drum / Dragon Dance / Geomancy / Swords Dance / Tail Glow)
featureDefinitions.winCondition: the team's late-game closer (setup sweeper or a weather/TR-boosted nuke)
featureDefinitions.hazardSetter: sets Stealth Rock / Spikes (meaningful in 6v6, light — NOT dead as in 4v4 VGC)
featureDefinitions.dedicatedSupport: a LOW-OFFENSE mon built to enable allies (redirection / Fake Out / Wide Guard / ally ability / Intimidate / speed-control), NOT an attacker that merely carries a support tool (T-141)

baseArchetypes.name: Bulky Offense (Intimidate/Fake Out core)
baseArchetypes.description: The default doubles framework: an Intimidate + Fake Out support core blunts the opponent to open a safe turn for a setup sweeper or spread attackers, with Tailwind (or a Trick Room backup) as speed control. Offensively slanted but carries real bulk and a clear win condition.

baseArchetypes.name: Balance / Dual-Mode Goodstuffs
baseArchetypes.description: Flexible matchup-adaptive team of strong mons carrying two win conditions and two speed-control modes (fast Tailwind/terrain + slow Trick Room) over a bulky Intimidate/redirection backbone; the pilot commits to the mode that beats the opponent.

baseArchetypes.name: Hyper Offense / Goodstuffs
baseArchetypes.description: All-out damage race with minimal defense (the 'no Protect' builds): a fast spread lead pair, or Fake Out + Follow Me guaranteeing a setup sweeper's turn, backed by a priority win button and Tailwind so it KOs faster than the opponent can stabilise.

baseArchetypes.name: Redirection Support Offense
baseArchetypes.description: Support is the IDENTITY, not a side dish (corpus: 'Redirection Support Offense'): a dedicated support soaks attacks off a fragile win condition (Follow Me / Rage Powder or Lightning Rod / Storm Drain), buying free turns for a setup sweeper or glass-cannon nuke; chip / speed-control softens targets. The with-support counterpart to hyper_offense's 'no Protect' builds (T-141).

gimmicks.name: Weather Offense (Sun/Rain/Sand/Snow)
gimmicks.description: A weather setter powers its own side and cripples the opponent's (Swift Swim/Chlorophyll Speed, boosted nukes); support shields the fragile setter, and a weather-independent backup covers weather wars. Duration rocks + weather-Speed matter in 6v6.

gimmicks.name: Trick Room
gimmicks.description: Inverts the speed order so slow, high-power attackers move first. Runs one or (for reliability) two Trick Room setters + slow abusers, with redirection/protection to survive the setup turn; strong builds keep a Tailwind fallback.

gimmicks.name: Redirection Support Offense
gimmicks.description: A support mon soaks attacks off a fragile win condition (Follow Me / Rage Powder or Lightning Rod / Storm Drain), buying free turns for a setup sweeper or glass-cannon nuke; chip/paralysis softens targets.

gimmicks.name: Perish Trap / Trapping Control
gimmicks.description: A control gimmick (ALLOWED per Batch-3): a Shadow Tag trapper locks a key foe in place while Perish Song's 3-turn count grinds it down (it cannot switch out); Substitute/Protect stall the count. Entry requires BOTH the trapper AND a Perish Song core — a lone Shadow Tag mon is a support trapper, not a perish-trap TEAM (T-124; corpus: 5/12 DOU trap teams run the full combo, Gothitelle/Gengar learn both themselves).

gimmicks.name: Screens & Tailwind / Field Control
gimmicks.description: Temporary field advantage rather than raw damage: Aurora Veil / screens blunt hits so setup sweepers boost safely, while Tailwind flips the speed war; a priority/Taunt disruptor protects the setup turns.
